heading: Treatment of qualified long-term care insurance
label: 7702B Treatment of qualified long-term care insurance
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title

References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(1)(B), (2)(B)(i), is , . Title XVIII of the Act is classified generally to subchapter XVIII (§ 1395 et seq.) of chapter 7 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Section 1861(r)(1) of the Act is classified to . Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–191, title III, § 321(f)110 Stat. 2059Aug. 21, 1996“(1)General effective date.—“(A).—In generalExcept as provided in subparagraph (B), the amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending sections 106, 125, 807, and 4980B of this title, , Labor, and , The Public Health and Welfare] shall apply to contracts issued after .section 1167 of Title 29section 300bb–8 of Title 42December 31, 1996“(B).—Reserve methodThe amendment made by subsection (b) [amending ] shall apply to contracts issued after .section 807 of this titleDecember 31, 1997“(2).—Continuation of existing policiesIn the case of any contract issued before , which met the long-term care insurance requirements of the State in which the contract was sitused [sic] at the time the contract was issued—January 1, 1997“(A)such contract shall be treated for purposes of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 as a qualified long-term care insurance contract (as defined in section 7702B(b) of such Code), and“(B)services provided under, or reimbursed by, such contract shall be treated for such purposes as qualified long-term care services (as defined in section 7702B(c) of such Code).In the case of an individual who is covered on , under a State long-term care plan (as defined in section 7702B(f)(2) of such Code), the terms of such plan on such date shall be treated for purposes of the preceding sentence as a contract issued on such date which met the long-term care insurance requirements of such State.December 31, 1996“(3).—Exchanges of existing policiesIf, after the date of enactment of this Act [] and before , a contract providing for long-term care insurance coverage is exchanged solely for a qualified long-term care insurance contract (as defined in section 7702B(b) of such Code), no gain or loss shall be recognized on the exchange. If, in addition to a qualified long-term care insurance contract, money or other property is received in the exchange, then any gain shall be recognized to the extent of the sum of the money and the fair market value of the other property received. For purposes of this paragraph, the cancellation of a contract providing for long-term care insurance coverage and reinvestment of the cancellation proceeds in a qualified long-term care insurance contract within 60 days thereafter shall be treated as an exchange.Aug. 21, 1996January 1, 1998“(4).—Issuance of certain riders permittedFor purposes of applying sections 101(f), 7702, and 7702A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to any contract—“(A)the issuance of a rider which is treated as a qualified long-term care insurance contract under section 7702B, and“(B)the addition of any provision required to conform any other long-term care rider to be so treated,shall not be treated as a modification or material change of such contract.“(5).—Application of per diem limitation to existing contractsThe amount of per diem payments made under a contract issued on or before , with respect to an insured which are excludable from gross income by reason of section 7702B of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by this section) shall not be reduced under subsection (d)(2)(B) thereof by reason of reimbursements received under a contract issued on or before such date. The preceding sentence shall cease to apply as of the date (after ) such contract is exchanged or there is any contract modification which results in an increase in the amount of such per diem payments or the amount of such reimbursements.”July 31, 1996July 31, 1996

Long-Term Care Study Request, , , related to a study of the marketing and other effects of per diem limits on certain types of long-term care policies, and provided that if the National Association of Insurance Commissioners agreed to the study request by Congress, the Association would report the results of the study not later than 2 years after accepting the request.Pub. L. 104–191, title III, § 321(g)110 Stat. 2060Aug. 21, 1996

children.text: a qualified long-term care insurance contract shall be treated as an accident and health insurance contract,

amounts (other than policyholder dividends, as defined in section 808, or premium refunds) received under a qualified long-term care insurance contract shall be treated as amounts received for personal injuries and sickness and shall be treated as reimbursement for expenses actually incurred for medical care (as defined in section 213(d)),
any plan of an employer providing coverage under a qualified long-term care insurance contract shall be treated as an accident and health plan with respect to such coverage,

except as provided in subsection (e)(3), amounts paid for a qualified long-term care insurance contract providing the benefits described in subsection (b)(2)(A) shall be treated as payments made for insurance for purposes of section 213(d)(1)(D), and
a qualified long-term care insurance contract shall be treated as a guaranteed renewable contract subject to the rules of section 816(e).

children.heading: Qualified long-term care insurance contract
children.label: b Qualified long-term care insurance contract
A
children.text: the only insurance protection provided under such contract is coverage of qualified long-term care services,

children.label: B
children.text: such contract does not pay or reimburse expenses incurred for services or items to the extent that such expenses are reimbursable under title XVIII of the Social Security Act or would be so reimbursable but for the application of a deductible or coinsurance amount,

children.label: C
children.text: such contract is guaranteed renewable,

children.label: i
children.text: paid, assigned, or pledged as collateral for a loan, or

children.label: ii
children.text: borrowed,

children.label: E
children.text: all refunds of premiums, and all policyholder dividends or similar amounts, under such contract are to be applied as a reduction in future premiums or to increase future benefits, and

children.label: F
children.text: such contract meets the requirements of subsection (g).

children.heading: Per diem, etc. payments permitted
children.label: A Per diem, etc. payments permitted
children.text: A contract shall not fail to be described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (1) by reason of payments being made on a per diem or other periodic basis without regard to the expenses incurred during the period to which the payments relate.

children.heading: Special rules relating to medicare
children.label: B Special rules relating to medicare
i
children.text: Paragraph (1)(B) shall not apply to expenses which are reimbursable under title XVIII of the Social Security Act only as a secondary payor.

children.label: ii
children.text: No provision of law shall be construed or applied so as to prohibit the offering of a qualified long-term care insurance contract on the basis that the contract coordinates its benefits with those provided under such title.

children.heading: Refunds of premiums
children.label: C Refunds of premiums
children.text: Paragraph (1)(E) shall not apply to any refund on the death of the insured, or on a complete surrender or cancellation of the contract, which cannot exceed the aggregate premiums paid under the contract. Any refund on a complete surrender or cancellation of the contract shall be includible in gross income to the extent that any deduction or exclusion was allowable with respect to the premiums.

children.label: A
children.text: are required by a chronically ill individual, and

children.label: B
children.text: are provided pursuant to a plan of care prescribed by a licensed health care practitioner.

children.label: i
children.text: being unable to perform (without substantial assistance from another individual) at least 2 activities of daily living for a period of at least 90 days due to a loss of functional capacity,

children.label: ii
children.text: having a level of disability similar (as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary in consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services) to the level of disability described in clause (i), or

children.label: iii
children.text: requiring substantial supervision to protect such individual from threats to health and safety due to severe cognitive impairment.

children.label: i
children.text: Eating.

children.label: ii
children.text: Toileting.

children.label: iii
children.text: Transferring.

children.label: iv
children.text: Bathing.

children.label: v
children.text: Dressing.

children.label: vi
children.text: Continence.

children.heading: Maintenance or personal care services
children.label: 3 Maintenance or personal care services
children.text: The term “maintenance or personal care services” means any care the primary purpose of which is the provision of needed assistance with any of the disabilities as a result of which the individual is a chronically ill individual (including the protection from threats to health and safety due to severe cognitive impairment).

children.heading: Licensed health care practitioner
children.label: 4 Licensed health care practitioner
children.text: The term “licensed health care practitioner” means any physician (as defined in section 1861(r)(1) of the Social Security Act) and any registered professional nurse, licensed social worker, or other individual who meets such requirements as may be prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Aggregate payments in excess of limits
children.label: d Aggregate payments in excess of limits
A
children.text: the periodic payments received for any period under all qualified long-term care insurance contracts which are treated as made for qualified long-term care services for an insured, and

children.label: B
children.text: the periodic payments received for such period which are treated under section 101(g) as paid by reason of the death of such insured,

children.label: i
children.text: the dollar amount in effect for such period under paragraph (4), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the costs incurred for qualified long-term care services provided for the insured for such period, over

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate payments received as reimbursements (through insurance or otherwise) for qualified long-term care services provided for the insured during such period.

children.label: A
children.text: all persons receiving periodic payments described in paragraph (1) with respect to the same insured shall be treated as 1 person, and

children.label: B
children.text: the per diem limitation determined under paragraph (2) shall be allocated first to the insured and any remaining limitation shall be allocated among the other such persons in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe.

children.heading: Dollar amount
children.label: 4 Dollar amount
children.text: The dollar amount in effect under this subsection shall be $175 per day (or the equivalent amount in the case of payments on another periodic basis).

children.heading: Inflation adjustment
children.label: 5 Inflation adjustment
children.text: In the case of a calendar year after 1997, the dollar amount contained in paragraph (4) shall be increased at the same time and in the same manner as amounts are increased pursuant to section 213(d)(10).

children.heading: Periodic payments
children.label: 6 Periodic payments
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “periodic payment” means any payment (whether on a periodic basis or otherwise) made without regard to the extent of the costs incurred by the payee for qualified long-term care services.

children.heading: Treatment of coverage provided as part of a life insurance or annuity contract
children.label: e Treatment of coverage provided as part of a life insurance or annuity contract

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: This title shall apply as if the portion of the contract providing such coverage is a separate contract.

children.heading: Denial of deduction under section 213
children.label: 2 Denial of deduction under section 213
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under section 213(a) for any payment made for coverage under a qualified long-term care insurance contract if such payment is made as a charge against the cash surrender value of a life insurance contract or the cash value of an annuity contract.

children.heading: Portion defined
children.label: 3 Portion defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “portion” means only the terms and benefits under a life insurance contract or annuity contract that are in addition to the terms and benefits under the contract without regard to long-term care insurance coverage.

children.heading: Annuity contracts to which paragraph (1) does not apply
children.label: 4 Annuity contracts to which paragraph (1) does not apply
A
children.text: A trust described in section 401(a) which is exempt from tax under section 501(a).

children.label: i
children.text: purchased by a trust described in subparagraph (A),

children.label: ii
children.text: purchased as part of a plan described in section 403(a),

children.label: iii
children.text: described in section 403(b),

children.label: iv
children.text: provided for employees of a life insurance company under a plan described in section 818(a)(3), or

children.label: v
children.text: from an individual retirement account or an individual retirement annuity.

children.label: C
children.text: A contract purchased by an employer for the benefit of the employee (or the employee’s spouse).

children.heading: Treatment of certain State-maintained plans
children.label: f Treatment of certain State-maintained plans
A
children.text: an individual receives coverage for qualified long-term care services under a State long-term care plan, and

children.label: B
children.text: the terms of such plan would satisfy the requirements of subsection (b) were such plan an insurance contract,

children.label: A
children.text: which is established and maintained by a State or an instrumentality of a State,

children.label: B
children.text: which provides coverage only for qualified long-term care services, and

children.label: i
children.text: employees and former employees of a State (or any political subdivision or instrumentality of a State),

children.label: ii
children.text: the spouses of such employees, and

children.label: iii
children.text: individuals bearing a relationship to such employees or spouses which is described in any of subparagraphs (A) through (G) of section 152(d)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: the requirements of the model regulation and model Act described in paragraph (2),

children.label: B
children.text: the disclosure requirement of paragraph (3), and

children.label: C
children.text: the requirements relating to nonforfeitability under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Requirements of model regulation and Act
children.label: 2 Requirements of model regulation and Act
I
children.text: Section 7A (relating to guaranteed renewal or noncancellability), and the requirements of section 6B of the model Act relating to such section 7A.

children.label: II
children.text: Section 7B (relating to prohibitions on limitations and exclusions).

children.label: III
children.text: Section 7C (relating to extension of benefits).

children.label: IV
children.text: Section 7D (relating to continuation or conversion of coverage).

children.label: V
children.text: Section 7E (relating to discontinuance and replacement of policies).

children.label: VI
children.text: Section 8 (relating to unintentional lapse).

children.label: VII
children.text: Section 9 (relating to disclosure), other than section 9F thereof.

children.label: VIII
children.text: Section 10 (relating to prohibitions against post-claims underwriting).

children.label: IX
children.text: Section 11 (relating to minimum standards).

children.label: X
children.text: Section 12 (relating to requirement to offer inflation protection), except that any requirement for a signature on a rejection of inflation protection shall permit the signature to be on an application or on a separate form.

children.label: XI
children.text: Section 23 (relating to prohibition against preexisting conditions and probationary periods in replacement policies or certificates).

children.label: I
children.text: Section 6C (relating to preexisting conditions).

children.label: II
children.text: Section 6D (relating to prior hospitalization).

children.heading: Model provisions
children.label: i Model provisions
children.text: The terms “model regulation” and “model Act” mean the long-term care insurance model regulation, and the long-term care insurance model Act, respectively, promulgated by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (as adopted as of January 1993).

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: ii Coordination
children.text: Any provision of the model regulation or model Act listed under clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A) shall be treated as including any other provision of such regulation or Act necessary to implement the provision.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: iii Determination
children.text: For purposes of this section and section 4980C, the determination of whether any requirement of a model regulation or the model Act has been met shall be made by the Secretary.

children.heading: Disclosure requirement
children.label: 3 Disclosure requirement
children.text: The requirement of this paragraph is met with respect to any contract if such contract meets the requirements of section 4980C(d).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The requirements of this paragraph are met with respect to any level premium contract, if the issuer of such contract offers to the policyholder, including any group policyholder, a nonforfeiture provision meeting the requirements of subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: The nonforfeiture provision shall be appropriately captioned.

children.label: ii
children.text: The nonforfeiture provision shall provide for a benefit available in the event of a default in the payment of any premiums and the amount of the benefit may be adjusted subsequent to being initially granted only as necessary to reflect changes in claims, persistency, and interest as reflected in changes in rates for premium paying contracts approved by the appropriate State regulatory agency for the same contract form.

children.label: I
children.text: Reduced paid-up insurance.

children.label: II
children.text: Extended term insurance.

children.label: III
children.text: Shortened benefit period.

children.label: IV
children.text: Other similar offerings approved by the appropriate State regulatory agency.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: 5 Cross reference
children.text: For coordination of the requirements of this subsection with State requirements, see section 4980C(f).
